Module com.example._0tazo
Package com.example._0tazo.utilities

Class GameTimer

java.lang.Object
com.example._0tazo.utilities.GameTimer
public class GameTimer extends Object
Manages the two timer threads used during a Cincuentazo game:
  1. General timer â€” runs for the entire game, firing ITimerListener.onTick(int) every second so the controller can update the elapsed-time label in the UI.
  2. Machine turn timer â€” fires once after a random delay of 2â€“4 seconds (ITimerListener.onMachineTurnReady()) to simulate the computer "thinking", then fires again after 1â€“2 seconds (ITimerListener.onMachineDrawReady()) to simulate drawing.

Both threads are daemon threads so they stop automatically when the JavaFX application exits. The general timer can be paused and resumed (e.g. while a win/lose overlay is shown), and both timers can be stopped cleanly when the game ends.

This class has no JavaFX dependency â€” the listener implementation in the controller is responsible for calling Platform.runLater() before touching any UI node.

Typical usage from the controller:


 GameTimer timer = new GameTimer(this); // controller implements ITimerListener
 timer.startGeneralTimer();             // begin counting elapsed time

 // When a machine turn starts:
 timer.startMachineTurnTimer();
 // â†’ onMachineTurnReady() fires after 2â€“4 s  (controller plays the card)
 // â†’ onMachineDrawReady() fires after 1â€“2 s more (controller draws)

 // When the game ends:
 timer.stopAll();

    Returns a random integer between min and max inclusive.

    Resumes the general timer after a pauseGeneralTimer() call.
    void
    startGeneralTimer()
    Starts the general game timer, firing ITimerListener.onTick(int) every second until stopAll() or stopGeneralTimer() is called.
    void
    startMachineTurnTimer()
    Starts the machine turn timer sequence: Waits a random delay between 2 and 4 seconds, then fires ITimerListener.onMachineTurnReady(). Waits a further random delay between 1 and 2 seconds, then fires ITimerListener.onMachineDrawReady().
    void
    stopAll()
    Stops both timers.
    void
    stopGeneralTimer()
    Stops the general timer permanently.
    void
    stopMachineTurnTimer()
    Stops the machine turn timer if it is currently running.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameTimer

      public GameTimer(ITimerListener listener)
      Constructs a GameTimer with the given event listener.
      Parameters:
      listener - the ITimerListener that will receive timer callbacks; typically the game controller

  • Method Details

    • startGeneralTimer

      public void startGeneralTimer()
      Starts the general game timer, firing ITimerListener.onTick(int) every second until stopAll() or stopGeneralTimer() is called.

      If the timer is already running this method does nothing.

    • pauseGeneralTimer

      public void pauseGeneralTimer()
      Pauses the general timer. The elapsed time stops incrementing and ITimerListener.onTick(int) stops firing until resumeGeneralTimer() is called.

    • resumeGeneralTimer

      public void resumeGeneralTimer()
      Resumes the general timer after a pauseGeneralTimer() call.

    • stopGeneralTimer

      public void stopGeneralTimer()
      Stops the general timer permanently. The thread is interrupted and the elapsed time is reset to zero.

    • startMachineTurnTimer

      public void startMachineTurnTimer()
      Starts the machine turn timer sequence:
      1. Waits a random delay between 2 and 4 seconds, then fires ITimerListener.onMachineTurnReady().
      2. Waits a further random delay between 1 and 2 seconds, then fires ITimerListener.onMachineDrawReady().

      If a machine turn timer is already running it is stopped before starting the new one.

    • stopMachineTurnTimer

      public void stopMachineTurnTimer()
      Stops the machine turn timer if it is currently running. Any pending onMachineTurnReady or onMachineDrawReady callbacks will not fire after this call.

    • stopAll

      public void stopAll()
      Stops both timers. Should be called when the game ends or the application closes.

    • getElapsedSeconds

      public int getElapsedSeconds()
      Returns the total seconds elapsed since the general timer started.
      Returns:
      elapsed seconds

    • isGeneralRunning

      public boolean isGeneralRunning()
      Returns true if the general timer is currently running and not paused.
      Returns:
      true if ticking

    • isMachineTimerRunning

      public boolean isMachineTimerRunning()
      Returns true if the machine turn timer is currently active.
      Returns:
      true if a machine delay is in progress

    • randomDelay

      private int randomDelay(int min, int max)
      Returns a random integer between min and max inclusive.
      Parameters:
      min - minimum value
      max - maximum value
      Returns:
      a random int in [min, max]
